housing.summary: Limited direct discussion of on-campus housing, but graduate and international students face real challenges finding off-campus housing without US credit history or a co-signer. Some nearby options like Poplar Manor near West Campus get positive reviews for maintenance responsiveness. Durham broadly is recommended over surrounding suburbs like Cary or Morrisville.
key_quotes: I've been here 4 years and it has all been great. Any issues were fixed within 24 hours
I am moving out of Poplar Manor (close to West Campus) at the end of June, so there will be a unit available
There's absolutely no reason to live outside of Durham if you will be at Duke

social_life.summary: Orientation programs like pArts and PCitizen are praised for building lasting friend groups, with many students staying close with their orientation cohort throughout college. Transfers specifically report it being very hard to find social groups and need to put in significant personal effort. The campus social scene seems structured around early bonding experiences.
key_quotes: my current friend group is almost all pArts kids; anecdotally i've noticed a lot of the pArts kids stay together even after orientation
I won't lie it was very hard for me [as a transfer]. You really need to put in effort yourself to find people
I loved PCitizen!! super fun DC trip with lots of cool activities, still great friends with the people from there even after graduating

dining.summary: There is virtually no discussion of dining halls or food quality in these posts. No meaningful data exists in this dataset to evaluate dining at Duke.
key_quotes: No relevant quotes available from these posts

mental_health.summary: No direct discussion of mental health resources or student wellbeing infrastructure appears in these posts. The academic pressure is implied by one senior reflecting that the hardest part of Duke was getting in, suggesting the workload may be more manageable than expected, but this is anecdotal.
key_quotes: the hardest part of this school was the application process
I didn't take the hardest course load possible, nor did I perform the greatest (3.84 GPA)

financial_aid.summary: Mixed signals on financial aid - some say it is comparable to peer schools while others warn it is worse than other elite privates, particularly for pre-med students. Duke Med has limited non-need-based scholarships, though MD/PhD students get full tuition plus a stipend. One prospective student explicitly noted 'their fin aid sucks compared to other privates.'
key_quotes: damn i would apply for premed, but their fin aid sucks compared to the other privates
I've had friends with ample support from the finaid office who did not get the same from other peer private schools
If you go the MD/PhD route, they cover all tuition, fees, plus pay you a modest stipend

academics.summary: Duke is known for grade inflation, with a 3.84 GPA described as 'just outside Latin Honors,' which surprised many commenters. The Focus program is highly sought after for freshmen, but many students are placed in the less-desired Constellations program despite writing all required essays. Duke notably does not offer undergraduate nursing despite its reputation in that area.
key_quotes: How the fuck is a 3.84 gpa not Latin honors lmao? Does duke have mad inflation that I'm not aware of
Duke definitely grade inflates and has been trending upward for years
Duke doesn't have nursing for undergraduates. That won't be available to you

administration.summary: The administration has faced serious backlash for revoking a promised lifetime alumni email, with many alumni calling it a broken promise and vowing not to donate. The response was seen as evasive and insulting, with critics saying Duke 'doubled down with weasel words.' Career services had explicitly encouraged graduates to use Duke emails for job applications, making the sudden revocation particularly disruptive.
key_quotes: I'm a recent grad and career services made a big deal about using our Duke.edu emails for job applications so I'm on more than 20 portals with that address
It isn't an issue. It's Duke being cheap, they just won't admit it
I will not be donating to Duke while this decision stands and I feel obligated to tell prospective students that the university does not value its commitments

location_and_campus.summary: Durham is generally well-regarded by students and described as unfairly stigmatized for safety concerns, particularly by those coming from expensive coastal cities. The campus itself is beautiful enough to be featured in professional 360-degree tour videos. Students from the Bay Area and California are specifically advised not to fear Durham and to live downtown rather than fleeing to suburbs like Cary.
key_quotes: Durham is not that unsafe
there is zero reason to live in cary or morrisville. i'm also from california (from the bay and lived in la for a decade) and i moved here two years ago
If you're a graduate student with SF money, I'd recommend downtown Durham until you know the area well enough

career_outcomes.summary: Duke graduates are recognized by the UK government's High Potential visa program as among the most elite American university alumni, signaling strong global career recognition. An Economics and CS senior reflected positively on career preparation, though they noted their GPA of 3.84 was not enough for Latin Honors due to grade inflation. Students interested in quant trading and finance appear drawn to Duke's programs.
key_quotes: Duke University is listed among the most elite American universities by the UK government for its high potential immigrant program
I am a current senior at Duke University majoring in Economics and Computer Science graduating next May
If you are super antsy [about career prep], look into some of the clubs

value_for_money.summary: Value is complicated - the brand carries significant prestige and global recognition, but financial aid is perceived by some as weaker than peer institutions, especially for pre-med students. Grade inflation means a high GPA is easier to achieve but may carry less signal to employers. The alumni email revocation also signals the university is willing to cut promised benefits, raising questions about long-term institutional loyalty.
key_quotes: damn i would apply for premed, but their fin aid sucks compared to the other privates
I feel obligated to tell prospective students that the university does not value its commitments
Duke definitely grade inflates and has been trending upward for years

overall_vibe.summary: Duke attracts driven, ambitious students interested in economics, CS, finance, and pre-professional paths, with a campus culture shaped heavily by orientation bonding experiences. The social scene rewards students who engage early, but transfers and those who miss out on programs like Focus can feel left out. The administration's handling of the alumni email issue has left a sour taste among some alumni and current students.
key_quotes: On the whole, Duke is a strikingly liberal campus
the hardest part of this school was the application process
Everyone ends up in one of their top 5 choices and orientation week is entirely what you make of it

red_flags.summary: The most concrete red flag is the administration revoking a promised lifetime alumni email with minimal notice and evasive justifications, actively alienating alumni and raising trust issues. Grade inflation is significant enough that a 3.84 GPA doesn't qualify for Latin Honors. Transfer students face genuine social isolation without strong institutional support systems.
key_quotes: Delaying a broken promise is still a broken promise. I will not be donating to Duke while this decision stands
The doubling down with weasel words is honestly insulting
I won't lie it was very hard for me [as a transfer]. You really need to put in effort yourself to find people

hidden_gems.summary: Duke's experiential orientation programs like pArts, PCitizen, and Project Wild are a genuine hidden strength, with students forming lasting friend groups that persist well beyond freshman year. The Focus program offers a rich intellectual community for those who get in. Duke's Box file storage has a quirky origin story - it was created by a Duke student - reflecting the university's quiet entrepreneurial culture.
key_quotes: my current friend group is almost all pArts kids; anecdotally i've noticed a lot of the pArts kids stay together even after orientation
I loved PCitizen!! super fun DC trip with lots of cool activities, still great friends with the people from there even after graduating
Box is not great, but it was created by a Duke student, and there is some loyalty to it
